prompt: If smooth surfaces exhibited full-spectrum absorption instead of reflection, draw a high-reflectivity planar surface facing a cylindrical vessel containing botanical specimens. The planar surface should be a full-spectrum absorbing void with no photon emission visible.

If planar reflective surfaces magnified optical inputs like concave refractive elements, draw a biological entity facing a planar surface. The reflected photon mapping should be significantly enlarged and zoomed in.

If liquid water exhibited full-spectrum absorption instead of reflection, draw a massive geological elevation by a large body of liquid water. The liquid surface should be a solid photon-absorbing void with no reflected photon mapping.

If high-reflectivity planar surfaces were unattenuated photon transmitters (transparent), draw a vehicle rear-facing reflective element. You should see the internal structure of the casing or the forward transparent silica barrier, not the rearward environment.

If silica-based transparent cladding absorbed all reflections, draw a massive architectural structure. The cladding should exhibit full-spectrum absorption with a matte finish, reflecting zero photons.

If liquid aqueous medium had a refractive index of 1.0, draw a cylindrical graphite-core implement partially submerged in a transparent vessel of the medium. The implement should appear perfectly linear with no refractive distortion at the phase boundary.

If liquid aqueous medium had the same refractive index as the atmospheric gas (no refraction), draw a metallic concave culinary implement partially submerged in a transparent vessel of the medium. The implement should appear perfectly linear and continuous.

If liquid aqueous medium had the same refractive index as the atmospheric gas (no refraction), draw a cylindrical hollow polymer tube with alternating pigmentation partially submerged in a transparent vessel of the medium. The tube should appear perfectly linear and continuous.

If liquid aqueous medium had the same refractive index as the atmospheric gas (no refraction), draw a bladed metallic cutting implement partially submerged in a transparent vessel of the medium. The implement should appear perfectly linear and continuous.

If the refractive index of liquid aqueous medium was inverted across the spectrum, draw a continuous spectral dispersion band in the atmosphere. The short-wavelength visible radiation (violet) should be on the outer edge and the long-wavelength visible radiation (red) on the inner edge.

If silica-based transparent medium had a refractive index of 1.0 (same as atmospheric gas), draw a coherent monochromatic mid-wavelength radiation beam passing through a silica block. The beam should travel in a perfectly linear path without angular deviation at the phase boundaries.

If a crystalline carbon lattice had the low refractive index of standard silica glass, draw a crystalline carbon lattice mounted on a toroidal metallic band under broad-spectrum radiation. The lattice should exhibit unattenuated photon transmission with zero internal total reflection or spectral dispersion.

If liquid aqueous medium had a negative refractive index, draw a cylindrical hollow polymer tube in a transparent vessel of the medium. The submerged portion of the tube should appear to undergo a sharp angular inversion relative to the unsubmerged portion.

If the refractive index was constant for all wavelengths (zero dispersion), draw broad-spectrum visible radiation intersecting a triangular dispersive medium. A single beam of unseparated broad-spectrum radiation should exit the medium.

If the convex refractive element had a refractive index of 1.0, draw the element positioned over a planar geographical data matrix. The visual data mapping transmitted through the element should be identical in scale to the external mapping.

If the core of a total internal reflection waveguide had a lower refractive index than its cladding, draw the waveguide transmitting photons. The photons should be leaking out of the lateral boundaries of the waveguide into the surrounding atmospheric gas.

If liquid aqueous medium had a refractive index of 1.0, draw a hemispherical aqueous droplet on a photosynthetic botanical appendage. The botanical vascular tissue transmitted through the droplet should be identical in scale to the external tissue, with zero optical magnification.

If the solid cylindrical silica-based element and the surrounding lipid-based liquid medium had identical refractive indices, draw the element submerged in the liquid. The submerged portion of the element should exhibit unattenuated photon transmission, rendering it optically undetectable.

If liquid aqueous medium matched the refractive index of a translucent surface-scattering silica pane, draw the silica pane coated with the liquid. The coated regions should exhibit unattenuated photon transmission, becoming optically clear.

If thermal gradients did not alter the refractive index of atmospheric gas, draw the optical transmission through the gas above an exothermic oxidation reaction. The transmitted background photon mapping should be perfectly linear and stable, with zero fluctuating refractive distortion.

If the convex refractive element had a refractive index of 1.0, draw the element exposed to broad-spectrum stellar radiation. There should be zero photon convergence at a focal point, only a photon-occluded dark region corresponding to the structural rim.
If photons propagated in helical trajectories, draw a multi-lens optical magnification system. The cylindrical housing of the system should be structurally deformed into a continuous helical spiral.

If photon-occluded dark regions retained the spectral reflection properties of the occluding object, draw a spherical biological fruit reflecting long-wavelength radiation casting an occlusion region. The occluded region on the horizontal surface should emit intense long-wavelength visible radiation (red).

If photon occlusion exhibited a significant temporal propagation delay, draw a biological entity executing rapid terrestrial locomotion. The photon-occluded dark region should be spatially displaced far behind the entity, completely detached from its pedal contact points.

If the propagation velocity of photons was drastically reduced, draw the celestial sphere emitting distant point-source visible radiation. The point sources should appear as elongated linear photon streaks due to the rotational velocity of the planetary mass.

If the exothermic oxidation of carbon emitted the spectral signature of copper, draw the oxidation of cellulose. The emitted plasma radiation should consist of intense short-wavelength (blue) and mid-wavelength (green) visible radiation.

If spectral dispersion was non-existent, draw broad-spectrum visible radiation intersecting a triangular dispersive medium. A single beam of unseparated broad-spectrum radiation should exit the medium.

If the atmospheric gas mixture preferentially scattered long-wavelength visible radiation instead of short-wavelength radiation, draw the atmospheric optical state during peak solar irradiation. The atmosphere should emit intense long-wavelength visible radiation (deep red).

If full-spectrum absorbing surfaces instead exhibited full-spectrum reflection, draw a hydrocarbon-aggregate terrestrial surface under high solar irradiation. The surface should emit blindingly intense broad-spectrum visible radiation.

If silica-based transparent medium was completely opaque to visible spectrum photons, draw a planar silica barrier. The barrier should exhibit full-spectrum absorption, appearing as a solid photon-absorbing void.

If coherent monochromatic radiation underwent instantaneous extreme spatial divergence, draw a focused coherent radiation spot on a vertical structural surface. The radiation should appear as a massive, low-intensity, highly diffuse photon scattering covering the entire surface.

If photosynthetic pigments absorbed mid-wavelength visible radiation, draw a photosynthetic botanical appendage. The appendage should exhibit near full-spectrum absorption, appearing as a photon-occluded dark void or deep short-wavelength/long-wavelength mixture.

If photons propagated in alternating angular trajectories, draw the photon-occluded dark region of a vertical linear barrier structure. The boundaries of the occluded region should be highly jagged and angularly alternating.

If pigments that typically reflect long-wavelength radiation instead reflected short-wavelength radiation, draw an octagonal traffic control marker. The marker should emit intense short-wavelength visible radiation (bright blue).
If photons propagated in helical trajectories, draw a directional beam of broad-spectrum stellar radiation. The photon beam should exhibit a continuous helical spiral morphology.

If expanding directional radiation beams instead underwent spatial convergence, draw an active portable directional radiation emitter. The emitted photon beam should narrow continuously to a singular focal point.
